topical_tags.term: Three years and a day (bat shalosh shanim veyom echad)
Kiddushin (betrothal/marriage)
Mitzas Hashana (part of a year counts as the whole)
Hymen/Besulim (virginity, rupture and reformation)
Niddah (menstrual impurity)
Issur Kehunah (forbidden to marry a kohen)
Justinian/Justina (non-Jewish woman challenging the halacha)
Moch (sponge/contraceptive device)
Sakana (danger to life)
Shomer Petaim Hashem (God protects the foolish)
Rabbi Akiva (Tanna disputing validity of sex under age three)
Nine years and a day (ben tisha shanim veyom echad)
Yibbum (levirate marriage, brother-in-law marriage)
Tumah (spiritual impurity from relations with niddah)
Maamar (verbal act of betrothal with sister-in-law)
Get (writ of divorce)
Chalitzah (shoe-removal ceremony to sever levirate bond)
Trumah (priestly dues; woman married to non-adult cannot eat)
Bat Mitzvah/Bar Mitzvah (age of majority at 12/13)
Mufla (one year before majority, when vows are checked)
Halacha Lmoshe Misinai (law from Moses at Sinai; absolute tradition)
Neder (vow)
Hekdesh (sanctification of objects)
Daas (intention, understanding, knowledge)
Shoteh (mentally unstable person)
